title: Best Dog Brushes by Coat Type (2026) – Slicker, Bristle & Deshedding
seoTitle: Best Dog Brushes by Coat Type 2026 – Expert Picks | GetPawsy
seoDescription: Find the best dog brush for your dog's coat type. Slicker, bristle, deshedding tools and rubber curry brushes compared for short, double, curly and long coats.
excerpt: Different coats need different brushes. This guide matches the right brush to your dog's coat type — whether short and smooth, double-coated, curly, or long and silky.
keywords: best dog brush by coat type

sections.heading: Why Coat Type Matters for Brushing
sections.content:
Using the wrong brush on your dog's coat is like using a fork to eat soup — it doesn't work and can cause damage. Each coat type has specific needs:

- **Short coats** need gentle tools that remove loose hair without irritating skin
- **Double coats** need tools that reach the dense undercoat without cutting the topcoat
- **Curly coats** need detangling tools to prevent painful matting
- **Long coats** need gentle pins that glide through without breaking fine hair

The right brush makes grooming faster, more comfortable for your dog, and far more effective at reducing shedding. Browse our full [dog brush collection](/collections/best-dog-brushes) to find your match.

sections.heading: Best Brushes for Short-Coated Dogs
sections.content:
**Breeds:** Beagles, Boxers, Dachshunds, Pugs, Great Danes, Pit Bulls

Short-coated dogs shed more than most people expect. The right tools:

### Rubber Curry Brush (Best Choice)
A rubber curry brush massages the skin while loosening dead hair. Use in circular motions before bathing for maximum effect. Dogs love the massage sensation.

### Bristle Brush
A natural or nylon bristle brush smooths the coat and distributes natural oils for a healthy shine. Use after the curry brush.

### Grooming Glove
For dogs that hate brushes, a grooming glove lets you "pet" the loose hair away. Less effective than a curry brush but better than nothing.

**Frequency:** Weekly brushing is sufficient for most short-coated breeds.

For complete grooming setups, check our [grooming kits](/collections/best-dog-grooming-kits). For the full grooming guide, read our [dog grooming tools guide](/guides/dog-grooming-tools-guide).

sections.heading: Best Brushes for Double-Coated Dogs
sections.content:
**Breeds:** Golden Retrievers, Huskies, German Shepherds, Labrador Retrievers, Corgis

Double-coated breeds have a dense, insulating undercoat that sheds heavily during spring and fall.

### Deshedding Tool (Best Choice)
Stainless steel deshedding tools reach through the topcoat to grab loose undercoat without cutting. This is the single most effective tool for reducing household shedding — up to 80% less fur on your furniture.

### Undercoat Rake
An undercoat rake with rotating pins is gentler than a deshedding tool and works well for dogs with sensitive skin.

### Slicker Brush
A slicker brush removes surface tangles and debris. Use it after the deshedding tool for a smooth finish.

**Important:** Never shave a double-coated breed. Their undercoat provides insulation in both heat and cold.

**Frequency:** 2-3 times per week, daily during heavy shedding seasons.

Need the full breakdown? Read the [complete grooming tools guide](/guides/dog-grooming-tools-guide).

sections.heading: Best Brushes for Curly and Wavy Coats
sections.content:
**Breeds:** Poodles, Doodles, Bichon Frisé, Portuguese Water Dogs

Curly coats don't shed much but mat easily — and mats can cause skin irritation and infection if left untreated.

### Slicker Brush (Best Choice)
A slicker brush with fine, flexible pins is essential for preventing and removing mats. Brush in sections, working from the ends toward the skin.

### Metal Comb
After brushing, run a metal comb through the coat to check for hidden mats. If the comb glides through, you're done.

### Detangling Spray
A leave-in detangling spray makes brushing easier and reduces hair breakage. Spray lightly before brushing.

**Frequency:** Daily or every other day. Curly coats mat quickly — consistency is key.

For all the tools in one purchase, browse our [grooming kits](/collections/best-dog-grooming-kits).

faq.question: What brush is best for a shedding dog?
faq.answer: A deshedding tool with stainless steel teeth is most effective. It removes up to 80% of loose undercoat without cutting the topcoat. Use 2-3 times per week.

faq.question: Can I use a human brush on my dog?
faq.answer: No. Human brushes have different bristle spacing and stiffness. Dog brushes are designed for canine coat types and skin sensitivity.

faq.question: How do I know if I'm brushing too hard?
faq.answer: If your dog flinches, whines, or tries to move away, you're pressing too hard. The brush should glide through the coat. Let the tool do the work.

featuredSnippet: The best dog brush depends on coat type: rubber curry brush for short coats, deshedding tool for double coats, slicker brush for curly coats, and pin brush for long silky coats. Match the brush to your dog's coat type for effective grooming.
